description_long: This is a small, ornately decorated ceramic vase or jug with a deep cobalt blue body and intricate white relief designs. The vase features a rounded form with a pronounced spout and a single, curved handle adorned with a beaded texture. Around the upper body, a garland of white floral motifs encircles the vessel, while the main panels display classical Greco-Roman figures in white, framed by symmetrical, stylized foliage. The base and shoulder are bordered with delicate white leaf and vine patterns, enhancing the neoclassical aesthetic. The interior is a plain, unglazed off-white, contrasting with the vivid blue and white exterior. The overall craftsmanship and decorative style suggest a piece inspired by or similar to Wedgwood Jasperware.
description: A small, cobalt blue ceramic vase with white neoclassical reliefs, a curved handle, and ornate floral and figure motifs. The vessel has a rounded body, spout, and detailed decorative borders.
description_view_0: This view highlights the handle's beaded texture and a partial classical figure in white relief, with the floral garland and decorative borders clearly visible.
description_view_1: The spout and a full classical figure in white relief are prominent, along with the ornate handle and symmetrical foliage framing the scene.
description_view_2: The back of the vase shows the handle's attachment and a continuation of the floral garland, with less emphasis on the relief figures.
description_view_3: This side displays another classical figure holding an object, framed by decorative foliage and bordered by the white garland and leaf patterns.

license_info.creator_display_name: Yale Peabody Museum of Natural History
